title_en: Taharah in Nur al-Idah: Purification for the Beginner
content_en:
Ash-Shurunbulali's treatment of taharah in Nur al-Idah presents the Hanafi purification law in the clear, accessible format that has made this text a standard teaching tool for beginners. The rulings are presented without excessive technical vocabulary, making them suitable for students who are approaching fiqh for the first time.

Water is classified in Nur al-Idah according to the Hanafi three-tier system: tahur (pure and purifying), tahir (pure but not purifying), and najis (impure). The student learns which types of water fall into each category and what consequences flow from each category for ritual use. The Maraqi al-Falah commentary expands on each point with the evidential basis and the reasoning.

The four obligatory acts of wudu are presented clearly: (1) washing the face, including the mouth and nose (madmadah and istinshaq are obligatory in the Hanafi school), (2) washing the arms including the elbows, (3) wiping at least one-quarter of the head, and (4) washing the feet including the ankles. The student learns each act and its boundaries with practical clarity.

The sunnah acts of wudu are listed separately so the student understands which elements are obligatory and which are additional: saying bismillah, washing the hands before beginning, rinsing the mouth and nose three times (beyond the obligatory single rinse), wiping the full head, wiping the ears and neck, and washing each member three times rather than the obligatory once.

The nullifiers of wudu are presented with the Hanafi school's characteristic list: anything exiting from the two passages, blood, pus, or vomit exiting from the body in sufficient quantity, loss of consciousness, and sleeping lying down (which is treated as potentially causing the loss of wudu due to the possibility of gas emission during deep sleep). The Hanafi position on sleeping and its effect on wudu — that sleeping in a position where gas could easily escape nullifies wudu, while sitting upright with a stable posture does not — is explained with practical application.

Ghusl is treated with its three obligatory acts: rinsing the mouth, rinsing the nose, and washing the entire body. Ash-Shurunbulali presents the occasions requiring ghusl: sexual intercourse (with or without ejaculation), ejaculation due to any cause, menstruation (at its conclusion), and postnatal bleeding (at its conclusion). The recommended manner of ghusl — following the prophetic method described in the hadiths of 'A'ishah — is presented after the obligatory minimum.

For the removal of najasah (physical impurity) from body, clothing, and prayer space, Nur al-Idah provides practical guidance. The general principle is that najasah must be removed until no trace remains that can be detected by sight or smell. For liquid impurities, three washes are the standard; for solid impurities, one thorough removal suffices. The Hanafi school's unique ruling that dry ground purified by drying in the sun can be prayed upon (even if not washed) is noted for its practical importance.
title_id: Taharah dalam Nur al-Idah: Bersuci untuk Pemula
content_id:
Pembahasan asy-Shurunbulali tentang taharah dalam Nur al-Idah menyajikan hukum bersuci Hanafi dalam format yang jelas dan mudah diakses yang telah menjadikan teks ini sebagai alat pengajaran standar bagi pemula. Hukum-hukum disajikan tanpa kosakata teknis yang berlebihan, membuatnya cocok bagi pelajar yang baru pertama kali mempelajari fikih.

Air diklasifikasikan dalam Nur al-Idah menurut sistem tiga tingkat Hanafi: tahur (suci dan menyucikan), tahir (suci tetapi tidak menyucikan), dan najis (tidak suci). Pelajar mempelajari jenis-jenis air mana yang termasuk dalam setiap kategori dan konsekuensi apa yang mengalir dari setiap kategori untuk penggunaan ritual. Komentar Maraqi al-Falah memperluas setiap poin dengan dasar bukti dan penalarannya.

Empat rukun wudu disajikan dengan jelas: (1) membasuh wajah, termasuk mulut dan hidung (berkumur dan memasukkan air ke hidung adalah wajib dalam mazhab Hanafi), (2) membasuh lengan termasuk siku, (3) mengusap setidaknya seperempat kepala, dan (4) membasuh kaki termasuk mata kaki. Pelajar mempelajari setiap rukun dan batas-batasnya dengan kejelasan praktis.

Sunnah-sunnah wudu dicantumkan secara terpisah agar pelajar memahami unsur-unsur mana yang wajib dan mana yang tambahan: membaca bismillah, mencuci tangan sebelum memulai, berkumur dan memasukkan air ke hidung tiga kali (melampaui pembilasan wajib sekali), mengusap seluruh kepala, mengusap telinga dan leher, serta mencuci setiap anggota tiga kali bukan yang wajib sekali.

Pembatal-pembatal wudu disajikan dengan daftar khas mazhab Hanafi: segala sesuatu yang keluar dari dua jalan, darah, nanah, atau muntah yang keluar dari tubuh dalam jumlah yang cukup, hilang kesadaran, dan tidur berbaring (yang dianggap berpotensi menyebabkan hilangnya wudu karena kemungkinan keluarnya gas selama tidur nyenyak). Posisi Hanafi tentang tidur dan pengaruhnya terhadap wudu - bahwa tidur dalam posisi di mana gas bisa mudah keluar membatalkan wudu, sementara duduk tegak dengan postur yang stabil tidak - dijelaskan dengan penerapan praktis.

Ghusl dibahas dengan tiga rukun wajibnya: berkumur, memasukkan air ke hidung, dan membasuh seluruh tubuh. Asy-Shurunbulali menyajikan kesempatan-kesempatan yang mengharuskan ghusl: hubungan seksual (dengan atau tanpa ejakulasi), ejakulasi karena sebab apa pun, haid (saat berakhir), dan nifas (saat berakhir). Cara ghusl yang dianjurkan - mengikuti metode kenabian yang digambarkan dalam hadis-hadis 'Aisyah - disajikan setelah minimum yang wajib.

Untuk menghilangkan najis (najasah fisik) dari tubuh, pakaian, dan tempat salat, Nur al-Idah memberikan panduan praktis. Prinsip umumnya adalah najis harus dihilangkan hingga tidak ada bekas yang dapat dideteksi oleh penglihatan atau penciuman. Untuk najis cair, tiga kali pencucian adalah standar; untuk najis padat, satu kali penghilangan yang menyeluruh sudah cukup. Hukum unik mazhab Hanafi bahwa tanah kering yang disucikan dengan pengeringan di bawah sinar matahari dapat dijadikan tempat salat (meskipun tidak dicuci) dicatat karena pentingnya secara praktis.
content_ar: Ash-Shurunbulali's treatment of الطهارة in Nur al-Idah presents the الحنفي purification law in the clear, accessible format that has made this text a standard teaching tool for beginners. The rulings are presented without excessive technical vocabulary, making them suitable for students who are approaching الفقه for the أولاً time. Water is classified in Nur al-Idah وفقاً لـ the الحنفي three-tier system: tahur (pure and purifying), tahir (pure but not purifying), and نجس (impure). The student learns which types of water fall into each category and what consequences flow from each category for ritual use. The Maraqi al-Falah commentary expands on each point with the evidential basis and the reasoning. The four obligatory acts of الوضوء are presented clearly: (1) washing the face, بما في ذلك the mouth and nose (madmadah and istinshaq are obligatory in the الحنفي school), (2) washing the arms بما في ذلك the elbows, (3) wiping at least one-quarter of the head, and (4) washing the feet بما في ذلك the ankles. The student learns each act and its boundaries with practical clarity. The السنة acts of الوضوء are listed separately لذا the student understands which elements are obligatory and which are additional: saying بسم الله, washing the hands قبل beginning, rinsing the mouth and nose three times (beyond the obligatory single rinse), wiping the full head, wiping the ears and neck, and washing each member three times rather than the obligatory حين. The nullifiers of الوضوء are presented with the الحنفي school's characteristic list: anything exiting from the two passages, blood, pus, or vomit exiting from the الجسد in sufficient quantity, loss of consciousness, and sleeping lying down (which is treated as potentially causing the loss of الوضوء بسبب the possibility of gas emission خلال deep sleep). The الحنفي position on sleeping and its effect on الوضوء — that sleeping in a position where gas could easily escape nullifies الوضوء, بينما sitting upright with a stable posture does not — is explained with practical application. الغسل is treated with its three obligatory acts: rinsing the mouth, rinsing the nose, and washing the entire الجسد. Ash-Shurunbulali presents the occasions requiring الغسل: sexual intercourse (with or without ejaculation), ejaculation بسبب any cause, menstruation (at its conclusion), and postnatal bleeding (at its conclusion). The recommended manner of الغسل — following the prophetic method described in the hadiths of 'A'ishah — is presented بعد the obligatory minimum. For the removal of النجاسة (physical impurity) from الجسد, clothing, and الصلاة space, Nur al-Idah provides practical الهداية. The general principle is that النجاسة must be removed حتى no trace remains that can be detected by sight or smell. For liquid impurities, three washes are the standard; for solid impurities, one thorough removal suffices. The الحنفي school's unique ruling that dry ground purified by drying in the sun
